title: Nematolepis squamea
intro: Nematolepis squamea, commonly known as Spiny Bitter Pea or Bitter Pea, is a shrub native to eastern Australia. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and cold. The bioactive constituents of Nematolepis squamea include alkaloids, glycosides, and other compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and other ailments.
taxonomy: Nematolepis squamea is classified within the family Cunoniaceae, which is a family of flowering plants native to Australia and New Zealand. This family is further grouped into the order Oxalidales, which includes other plant families such as the Oxalidaceae. The Cunoniaceae family is characterized by its woody plants with simple or compound leaves. Nematolepis squamea itself is a shrub or small tree that is endemic to Western Australia.
